note: Distilled from Shopify Dev MCP (search_docs_chunks + learn_shopify_api). Each family is a dense, imperative brief of CURRENT platform constraints. Refresh via apps/web/scripts/build-shopify-docs-snapshot.ts. tokenEstimate is a coarse chars/4 heuristic.

theme.docBlock: Theme sections & app blocks (Online Store, Liquid). Sections live in sections/*.liquid; theme app extensions ship blocks/*.liquid + snippets/ + assets/. Every section/block needs a {% schema %} JSON tag. Section schema keys: name, settings, blocks, max_blocks, presets, tag, class, limit, enabled_on/disabled_on. presets make the section appear in the theme editor Add-section picker. App blocks set target:"section"; app EMBED blocks target "body"/"head" and have settings but no presets. Input setting types: text, textarea, richtext, inline_richtext, html, liquid, number, range (requires min/max/step, optional unit; default must be numeric within range), checkbox (boolean), radio, select (both require options[]), color, color_scheme, color_background, font_picker, image_picker, video, video_url, url, product, product_list, collection, collection_list, blog, article, page, link_list, text_alignment, metaobject, metaobject_list. Read values via section.settings.<id> / block.settings.<id>. Put {{ block.shopify_attributes }} on each block root for editor drag-and-drop. Allow nested blocks with {"type":"@theme"} and {"type":"@app"}. Wrap all merchant-visible copy in {{ 'key' | t }} and add keys to locales/en.default.json. Liquid rules: no parentheses in conditions, no ternary operator, for-loops cap at 50 iterations (use {% paginate %}), {% render %} isolates scope so pass every variable as a param, use image_url/image_tag (never deprecated img_url/img_tag). Per-component CSS/JS goes in {% stylesheet %}/{% javascript %} (Liquid is NOT evaluated inside those tags).

checkoutUi.docBlock: Checkout UI extensions (Checkout, Thank-you, Order-status), API 2026-01. Runtime is a sandboxed web worker, NOT the DOM: no window/document, no direct DOM access. Build UI only from Polaris web components (s-* tags: s-banner, s-stack, s-box, s-button, s-text, s-text-field, s-checkbox, s-choice-list, s-select, s-image). They are globally registered — no import. Entry point: import '@shopify/ui-extensions/preact'; then render(...) from 'preact'. Use camelCase attributes (paddingBlock, not padding-block). Never include HTML comments. Placement = a target declared in shopify.extension.toml [[extensions.targeting]], e.g. purchase.checkout.block.render, purchase.checkout.cart-line-item.render-after, purchase.checkout.contact.render-after, purchase.checkout.payment-method-list.render-before, purchase.checkout.shipping-option-list.render-after, purchase.checkout.delivery-address.render-after, purchase.thank-you.block.render, customer-account.order-status.block.render. The target decides which components and APIs are valid. Capabilities are opt-in under [extensions.capabilities]: api_access (Storefront API), network_access (external fetch), collect_buyer_consent (SMS/marketing consent via s-consent-checkbox), block_progress (block the buyer advancing). Only checkout targets can WRITE cart metafields (applyMetafieldChange); thank-you and order-status targets are read-only. Prefer Shopify data APIs (appMetafields, metaobjects, settings, localization) over network calls — every load-time fetch adds latency and shows a loading state; extensions run async and independently of the page. Merchant [extensions.settings]: max 20 per definition, all optional — degrade gracefully when a setting is unset. Full checkout-page editing requires Checkout Extensibility (Shopify Plus).

postPurchase.docBlock: Post-purchase offer extension: a single-page app shown AFTER payment but BEFORE the Thank-you page. It uses the dedicated post-purchase API (@shopify/post-purchase-ui-extensions / -react), NOT checkout Polaris s-* components. Two targets: Checkout::PostPurchase::ShouldRender (decide whether to render; preload+store offer data) and Checkout::PostPurchase::Render (the offer UI). Components: CalloutBanner, BlockStack, Layout, Tiles, Button, Text, TextContainer, Image, Separator, Select, BuyerConsent. Hard constraints: only ONE post-purchase page per app, and it IS a single-page app (paginate inside it for multiple steps). Only one app can be the store's active post-purchase app. A customer can accept at most 3 consecutive offers per checkout — show 3 or fewer. Renders only for orders placed through the Online Store channel; order total must be >= $0.50. It is NOT shown when the buyer paid with a wallet or installment method (Shop Pay, Apple/Google/Amazon Pay, Klarna, Affirm, Afterpay), a gift card, or any non-credit-card method; when there is no shipping address and you try to add a subscription; for local delivery; or for orders with duties/multiple currencies. Apply order changes with calculateChangeset({changes}) then applyChangeset({changes}); collect BuyerConsent for subscription policies. The header and footer are fixed and cannot be customized. Live-store use is gated behind an access request (beta).

discountFunction.docBlock: Discount Shopify Function. Use the SINGLE unified Discount API — the separate Order Discount, Product Discount, and Shipping Discount APIs are DEPRECATED; do not pick them. Targets: cart.lines.discounts.generate.run (product + order discounts) and cart.delivery-options.discounts.generate.run (shipping); optional .fetch targets require network_access. Read discount.discountClasses — an array of PRODUCT, ORDER, and/or SHIPPING that the merchant's discount enabled. Emit operations ONLY for classes present; if the class you need is absent, return { operations: [] }. Return CartLinesDiscountsGenerateRunResult { operations }: CartOperation::ProductDiscountsAdd or OrderDiscountsAdd, each with candidates[] { targets (ProductVariant / CartLine / OrderSubtotal), value: Percentage { value } or FixedAmount { amount }, message, selectionStrategy: First / Maximum / All }. The delivery target returns CartDeliveryOptionsDiscountsGenerateRunResult. combinesWith (orderDiscounts/productDiscounts/shippingDiscounts) lives on the merchant's discount node and is enforced by Shopify — the function only proposes candidates, it does not decide combinability. Functions are PURE: no network (except an explicit fetch target), no filesystem, no Date/time, no randomness — all data must come from the input GraphQL query (camelCase fields; union types require __typename). Store config in a jsonValue metafield read via discount.metafield(namespace, key). Percentage value is a number; FixedAmount amount is a money string. Scaffold: shopify app generate extension --template discount.

cartTransform.docBlock: Cart Transform Shopify Function — expands, merges, and updates cart lines and customizes their presentation (bundles, kits, componentized products). Single target: cart.transform.run (export cartTransformRun). Activated once per app via the cartTransformCreate mutation. Return { operations: [...] } or NO_CHANGES. Operations: lineExpand — split one cart line into expandedCartItems[] each { merchandiseId, quantity, price: { adjustment: { fixedPricePerUnit: { amount } } } } (for bundles). linesMerge — combine several lines into one parent { cartLines[], parentVariantId, price } (for grouping). lineUpdate — change an existing line's title/image/price presentation. Collision rules when multiple operations (or multiple installed apps) target the same line: operations are ordered by each app's cartTransformCreate activation time; per line only the FIRST lineExpand executes and the rest are discarded; lineExpand beats linesMerge beats lineUpdate; a fixed lineExpand beats a customized lineExpand. Minimize collisions in your logic. You CANNOT remove a cart line. PURE function: no network, no Date/time, no randomness; all data from the input GraphQL query; config via a jsonValue metafield. Prices are money strings; multiply by presentmentCurrencyRate for multi-currency. Scaffold: shopify app generate extension --template cart_transform.

deliveryPaymentFunction.docBlock: Checkout & delivery Shopify Functions: delivery customization, payment customization, cart/checkout validation, fulfillment constraints, order-routing location rule, and local-pickup / pickup-point generators. ALL are PURE: no network/filesystem/Date/time/randomness; every input comes from the function's GraphQL input query (camelCase; __typename on union types); configuration via a jsonValue metafield. Delivery Customization (run, optionally fetch+run): rename, reorder, hide, or sort the delivery/shipping options presented at checkout via operations — it cannot invent new rates. Payment Customization (run): rename, reorder, hide, or sort payment methods and set payment terms; enum placement values are Title-cased in Rust. Cart & Checkout Validation (cart.validations.generate.run): return errors[] { message, target } to block or annotate checkout — you validate only, you cannot mutate the cart. Fulfillment Constraints: declare must-fulfill-together / must-fulfill-from rules; Shopify performs the allocation. Order Routing Location Rule: rank or filter candidate fulfillment locations. Local Pickup and Pickup Point generators (fetch+run): GENERATE the pickup options offered at checkout. The returned JSON must exactly match that target's FunctionRunResult shape. Scaffold: shopify app generate extension --template <delivery_customization | payment_customization | cart_checkout_validation | fulfillment_constraints | order_routing_location_rule | local_pickup_delivery_option_generator | pickup_point_delivery_option_generator>.

adminExtension.docBlock: Admin UI extensions embed into the Shopify admin. API 2026-01 uses Polaris web components ONLY (s-* tags: s-admin-block, s-admin-action, s-admin-print-action, s-button, s-text-field, s-section, s-stack, s-banner); do NOT import React from @shopify/ui-extensions-react/admin (that path is only for the 2025-07 version). Entry: import '@shopify/ui-extensions/preact'; render(...) from 'preact'. camelCase attributes; never include HTML comments. Each extension is a target in shopify.extension.toml. Types (scaffold: shopify app generate extension --template <admin_block | admin_action | admin_link | admin_print>): admin_block — a card pinned inline on a resource page (target like admin.product-details.block.render); the merchant must add and pin it; root <s-admin-block>. admin_action — a modal launched from the More-actions or bulk-action menu (target like admin.product-details.action.render); root <s-admin-action> with primary/secondary action buttons. admin_link — a pure-TOML deep link from an admin resource page into your app (no UI code); the toml registration is the deploy. admin_print — a Print Action extension for the Print menu on orders/products; root <s-admin-print-action src>. Discount UI settings — a FunctionSettings extension at admin.discount-details.function-settings.render using <s-function-settings> to configure a paired discount Function's metafield. Customer-segment templates — target admin.customers.segmentation-templates.data returns pre-built segment query templates. Use the Should Render API to hide conditionally, and Resource Picker / Picker / Intents utility APIs. Read/write admin data through the Admin GraphQL API.

customerAccount.docBlock: Customer Account UI extensions (new customer accounts): Order index, Order status, and Profile pages. API 2026-01, Polaris web components (s-* tags), Preact entry (import '@shopify/ui-extensions/preact'; render from 'preact'). Scaffold: shopify app generate extension --template customer_account_ui. The buyer is AUTHENTICATED: use the Authenticated Account API and Customer Account API (session token) to read the logged-in customer and their orders; the Order Status API exposes order, cartLines, fulfillment, cost, discounts, addresses, and metafields. Some targets can requireLogin. Declare targets in [[extensions.targeting]]: customer-account.order-index.block.render, customer-account.order-status.block.render, customer-account.order-status.fulfillment-details.render-after, customer-account.order-status.customer-information.render-after, customer-account.profile.block.render, customer-account.profile.addresses.render-after, customer-account.order.action.render (+ .menu-item.render), full-page customer-account.page.render and customer-account.order.page.render, plus B2B customer-account.profile.company-* targets, and .announcement.render targets for banners. The target dictates which components and APIs are valid. Full-page targets compose <s-page>/<s-section>; order-action targets use <s-customer-account-action>; collect consent via s-consent-checkbox. Sandboxed — no DOM; render graceful empty and loading states.

flow.docBlock: Shopify Flow extensions (merchant automation): either a flow_trigger (starts a workflow) or a flow_action (a step Flow invokes). Configured ENTIRELY in shopify.extension.toml — there is no UI framework. Scaffold: shopify app generate extension and pick Flow Trigger or Flow Action. For custom apps these are Shopify Plus only. Common [[extensions]] props: api_version, name (merchant-facing, 5-30 chars; Flow allows only alphanumerics and spaces), handle (<=30 chars, unique across the app, alphanumeric+hyphens, immutable after first deploy), description. flow_trigger: type="flow_trigger"; [[settings.fields]] declare the payload you send Flow using reference types (customer_reference, order_reference, product_reference) and scalar types (single_line_text_field, multi_line_text_field, number_decimal, number_integer, boolean, url). Fire the trigger by calling the Admin GraphQL flowTriggerReceive mutation with the handle and payload. flow_action: type="flow_action" plus REQUIRED runtime_url (Flow POSTs the action payload there at execution time). Optional: validation_url, config_page_url, config_page_preview_url (a custom config page requires validation_url), and schema (a GraphQL file) with return_type_ref to return typed data to later workflow steps. Each field has key, name, type, required, description. Name fields as object + past-tense verb, sentence case, no punctuation (e.g. "Auction bid placed"). Your app must host the runtime_url and validation_url routes.

webPixel.docBlock: Web Pixel extension (analytics / behavioral tracking). In shopify.extension.toml set type="web_pixel_extension", runtime_context="strict" (app pixels load in a STRICT sandbox), and a [customer_privacy] block declaring analytics / marketing / preferences / sale_of_data. Register with: import { register } from '@shopify/web-pixels-extension'; register(({ analytics, browser, init, settings, customerPrivacy }) => { ... }). Sandboxed: NO direct DOM/window/document, and NO loading of arbitrary scripts. Reach the browser only through the async browser API (browser.cookie / localStorage / sessionStorage, executed in the top frame). Subscribe to customer events with analytics.subscribe('<event>', cb). Standard events include page_viewed, product_viewed, product_added_to_cart, checkout_started, checkout_contact_info_submitted, payment_info_submitted, checkout_completed, search_submitted, collection_viewed; subscribe in bulk with all_events, all_standard_events, all_custom_events, all_dom_events; publish custom events for anything unlisted. init.data is a snapshot of cart/customer at page load, init.context is the page context, and settings holds the JSON configured via the Admin webPixelCreate mutation (settings fields must be single_line_text_field only). Respect consent: read init.customerPrivacy and customerPrivacy.subscribe('visitorConsentCollected', cb) which yields { analyticsProcessingAllowed, marketingAllowed, preferencesProcessingAllowed, saleOfDataAllowed }; gate tracking on the relevant flag. Send data with fetch(url, { method: 'POST', keepalive: true }).
